title: podman failing to honor PATH variable setting in image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The `elastic/filebeat:7.6.1` image sets the `PATH` environment variable. When running under Docker this works correct, but when running under Podman, the environment variable is not set as expected.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `echo 'echo $PATH' | podman run --rm -i --entrypoint bash elastic/filebeat:7.6.1`

**Describe the results you received:**

The above test, with Podman, returns:

```
/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The same command, with Docker, includes the correct output:

```
$ echo 'echo $PATH' | docker run --rm -i --entrypoint bash elastic/filebeat:7.6.1 
/usr/share/filebeat:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.3-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
Git Commit:         c2f37afabbdeb7bb062f7c5211601c10f5e3f99f-dirty
Built:              Fri Aug  3 20:00:00 2018
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 16840126464

comments.body:
Here's a simple reproducer.

Use this Dockerfile:

```
FROM alpine
ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin
```

Build the image:

```
podman build -t issue5685 .
```

Spawn a shell and look at `$PATH`:

```
$ podman run -it --rm issue5685 sh
/ # echo $PATH
/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```
Thanks for the issue @larsks, I'll take a look a bit later today.

@larsks I'm not seeing this with the latest stable Podman version 1.8.2 on Fedora 31.  I also tried with upstream and didn't see it there.  I think you're running this on Fedora 31 too?  Can I ask where you got that version of Podman from?  Can you confirm if it works for you running rootless and as root?

This is really weird, we'd this problem a while back, like version 1.4 and we fixed, so this is vexing to me.

root run
```
# podman version
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

# cat ./Dockerfile
FROM alpine
ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin

# podman build -t tom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob aad63a933944 done  
Copying config a187dde48c [======================================] 1.5KiB / 1.5KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin
STEP 3: COMMIT tom
--> b0b5e20ec86
b0b5e20ec86ab17448c5018332138a6a8e384412eaf1620263723adedc2d28ff

# podman run -it --rm tom /bin/sh
/ # echo $PATH
/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin
/ # exit
```
Run as non-root user "tom"
```
$ podman build -t tom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob aad63a933944 done  
Copying config a187dde48c done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin
STEP 3: COMMIT tom
--> a9876af051d
a9876af051d6d805f364cf4636c094c610eb1278448b59225b990b6dd8230642

$ podman run -it --rm tom /bin/sh
/ # echo $PATH
/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin

```

I'm using the `podman` binary from @baude's COPR repository (which is built from `contrib/spec`). `podman version` reports:

```
Version:            1.8.3-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
Git Commit:         1db64249113bfd24e4efd33f4c23ca95ce2b4bc5-dirty
Built:              Fri Aug  3 20:00:00 2018
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

I see the same behavior running as `root` or non-`root`.
Here's a terminal session reproducing the problem: https://asciinema.org/a/Zcjwu5F5fO89C3eAOXrcBYUUY

```
./bin/podman build -t dan /tmpPATH
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin
--> Using cache f5588885e64311394f4e6091b6d563cc1e9873e0a29954d467777653f9982185
STEP 3: COMMIT dan
--> f5588885e64
f5588885e64311394f4e6091b6d563cc1e9873e0a29954d467777653f9982185
[dwalsh@localhost libpod]$ ./bin/podman run -ti dan sh
/ # echo $PATH
/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```

The problem seems to be in Buildah.
```
./bin/podman inspect dan | grep PATH
                "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin",
```
@rhatdan since I first saw this with an image built with docker (the elasticsearch filebeat image), it can't be a buildah problem, right?
@rhatdan, I didn't pull down the absolute latest upstream before testing, so it sounds like it might be a change in the past week or two maybe?
It looks like buildah  is not saving any Env from the Dockerfile right now.

@rhatdan  In fact, I can't reproduce the problem that you're seeing :).

```
$ podman image inspect issue5685 | grep PATH
                "PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin"
                "created_by": "/bin/sh -c #(nop) ENV PATH=/test/value:/usr/bin:/usr/local/bin",
```

This is running from 6d36d054. What I'm seeing is a runtime, not a build-time, problem.
Ok I am seeing what you are seeing there also. This seems to be in the master branch.  Probably related to container.conf patch set.
Thanks for the issue and updates @larsks , made life easier to track down.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Looks like this is fixed in master.
